brief_justification: Paper A is computational continuum solid mechanics: a hybrid material-point-method (Lagrangian FE + Eulerian FD) simulation of large-deformation bending and contact in slender elastic tubes and tape springs (Brazier instability), validated against physical three-point-bending experiments. Paper B is pure topological string theory / algebraic geometry: 3d mirror symmetry and SYZ transformations between hyperkahler intermediate Jacobians of a compact Calabi-Yau threefold, with Rozansky-Witten theories, derived-category A/B-branes, and DT theory. There is no shared governing equation, transition, or transferable process; the fields overlap in no mechanism, so no role-for-role mapping holds.
